effect_coverage.note: The 7 no-op effects are in VALID_CODES and pass validation, then fall into the default branch. They emit console.warn but are pushed to applied[]. The player receives no state change. UNLOCK_OGHAM is the most dangerous of these — any card carrying it silently fails to grant the ogham.

scaleAndCap_behavior.multiplier_0: Impossible per MULTIPLIER_TABLE (min factor is -1.5). If somehow passed, Math.abs(0)=0 produces 0-magnitude effects — state methods called with 0, functionally safe.
scaleAndCap_behavior.multiplier_negative_1_5_echec_critique: Math.abs(-1.5)=1.5 — effects hit at 1.5x magnitude. ADD_REPUTATION sign is handled separately at line 124. DAMAGE_LIFE and HEAL_LIFE always scale by absolute multiplier, meaning critical failure amplifies effect magnitude in both directions. This is intentional per MULTIPLIER_TABLE design (punishing failures hit harder).
scaleAndCap_behavior.multiplier_muin_invert: processOghamModifiers() rewrites effect strings (DAMAGE_LIFE <-> HEAL_LIFE, negate ADD_REPUTATION delta) BEFORE applyEffects() is called. The multiplier passed to applyEffects is the minigame score factor (positive). Muin inversion does not depend on multiplier sign — correctly separated concerns.
scaleAndCap_behavior.very_large_multipliers: EFFECT_CAPS enforced in scaleAndCap(). DAMAGE_LIFE caps at 15, HEAL_LIFE at 18, ADD_REPUTATION at +/-20, ADD_ANAM at 20 (explicit). No runaway values possible.
scaleAndCap_behavior.fulfill_promise_quirk: Reward of +5 anam is scaled by multiplier via scaleAndCap. At echec_critique (factor -1.5), Math.abs gives 1.5x, so reward is +7 anam rounded. A critical fail on a promise-fulfillment card gives MORE anam than a partial success (0.5x = +3). Minor but counterintuitive — player who barely survives a card gets less reward for keeping a promise than one who botches it spectacularly.

card_generation.template_source: Externalized to /data/cards.json (loadTemplates). Inline pool removed (noted as 186 templates / ~130KB).
card_generation.biome_filtering: generateFastRouteCard() filters by t.biome === biome. Falls back to full pool when biomePool.length < 3. Cards.json biome coverage cannot be verified from TypeScript source — depends on authoring quality of the JSON file.
card_generation.repeat_prevention: Ring buffer of 3 (RECENT_CARD_BUFFER) correctly prevents consecutive repeats. Fallback to full pool when filtered pool is tiny — no deadlock.
card_generation.emergency_fallback: EMERGENCY_TEMPLATE is narrative content ('Le brouillard se leve...') with 3 realistic options. Not a debug placeholder. Safe.
card_generation.biome_coverage_risk: If cards.json has no biome tags or missing tags for some of the 8 biomes, the biome pool will always be empty for those biomes, causing full-pool fallback every card. Player sees non-biome-thematic cards. Not a crash but a narrative break.

game_loop_safety.boot_screen: Promise.race([transitionend, setTimeout(1000)]) — cannot hang. T069 skip also works via click/keydown with { once: true } cleanup. Safe.
game_loop_safety.main_menu_await: await new Promise<void> on startBtn click — intentional user-gate, not a deadlock. RAF loop runs independently.
game_loop_safety.deadlock_risk: None detected in lines 1-120. No unconditional infinite await chain.
game_loop_safety.note: Full run loop (card draw -> minigame -> effects -> return) is beyond line 120 and could not be audited. Recommend a follow-up read of lines 120-300 to verify drain timing and the full await chain.

life_drain_timing.constants_correct: LIFE_DRAIN_PER_CARD=1, LIFE_DRAIN_STAGE2=2 at card 15, LIFE_DRAIN_STAGE3=3 at card 25. Consistent with CLAUDE.md pipeline step 1 (DRAIN before CARTE).
life_drain_timing.stage_escalation: HEAL_LIFE gets halved after card 20 (cardsPlayed >= 20, Math.floor(raw * 0.5), min 1). With stage2 drain=2, avg heal ~2.2 — net slightly positive. With stage3 drain=3, net -0.8 per card. Tension curve is correct.
life_drain_timing.late_game_heal_halving: Applied INSIDE applyEffects, using store.getState().run.cardsPlayed at apply time. Correct: reads live state, not stale snapshot.
life_drain_timing.drain_call_site: Not visible in lines 1-120. Cannot confirm the drain fires at step 1 of the pipeline from this audit. Unverified.

issues.issue: UNLOCK_OGHAM is a validated, parseable effect that silently does nothing
issues.detail: VALID_CODES declares UNLOCK_OGHAM:1. parseEffect() accepts it. applyEffects() falls into the default no-op branch with console.warn, then pushes it to applied[]. Any card carrying UNLOCK_OGHAM:ioho as a reward gives the player nothing. If the LLM or FastRoute templates emit this effect, the new ogham branch is never opened. The effect is registered as if it worked.
issues.recommendation: Implement UNLOCK_OGHAM in the switch statement to call store.getState().unlockOgham(args[0]) — or if the store method does not exist yet, add it. Alternatively, move UNLOCK_OGHAM to a separate post-effect handler in the game loop that is clearly marked as not-yet-implemented so cards cannot be authored with it until it is ready.

issues.issue: 5 other no-op effects (ADD_KARMA, ADD_TENSION, PROGRESS_MISSION, SET_FLAG, ADD_TAG, REMOVE_TAG) silently pass as applied
issues.detail: These 6 codes are validated and accepted, pushed to applied[], but change no state. A card author writing ADD_KARMA:5 or SET_FLAG:visited_well:true will see no error and no result. The only signal is console.warn which is invisible to players and easy to miss in production.
issues.recommendation: For each unimplemented code, either remove it from VALID_CODES (prevents accidental authoring), or add a comment block in the switch documenting explicitly which cycle will implement them. Consider a separate UNIMPLEMENTED_CODES registry that causes rejection (pushed to rejected[]) rather than silent no-op.

issues.issue: FULFILL_PROMISE anam reward scales inversely — echec_critique (factor 1.5) gives more anam than reussite_partielle (factor 0.5)
issues.detail: At line 159, the +5 anam reward for keeping a promise is scaled by Math.abs(multiplier). A minigame score of 0-20 (echec_critique, factor -1.5) gives +8 anam. A score of 51-79 (reussite_partielle, factor 0.5) gives +3 anam. The worst minigame performance yields the best moral reward, which is narratively incoherent and can be exploited.
issues.recommendation: Make the promise anam reward flat (unscaled), mirroring how BREAK_PROMISE damage is flat at line 172. Change: store.getState().addAnam(5) — no scaleAndCap call. The moral weight of keeping a promise should not depend on minigame skill.

balance_recommendations.current: scaleAndCap('ADD_ANAM', 5, multiplier) — varies 3-8 anam by score
balance_recommendations.suggested: flat addAnam(5) — mirrors BREAK_PROMISE flat damage design
balance_recommendations.rationale: Consistency with sibling effect, avoids counterintuitive inverse scaling

balance_recommendations.suggested: Implement store.unlockOgham(oghamId) or reject from VALID_CODES until implemented
balance_recommendations.rationale: Prevents silent failure of ogham unlock cards

balance_recommendations.suggested: Move to FUTURE_CODES (separate constant) that causes rejection or explicit skip with stricter logging
balance_recommendations.rationale: Prevents accidental card authoring with effects that do nothing

positive_findings: scaleAndCap() is robust — all numeric effect types have caps, Math.abs on multiplier prevents sign confusion in magnitude calculation
processOghamModifiers() correctly pre-processes effect strings before applyEffects(), keeping Muin invert and Tinne doubling cleanly separated from core apply logic
EMERGENCY_TEMPLATE is narrative content, not a debug placeholder — safe for players to encounter
Ring buffer repeat prevention (RECENT_CARD_BUFFER=3) correctly handles narrow biome pools
Boot screen Promise.race pattern is deadlock-safe and handles prefers-reduced-motion
Late-game HEAL_LIFE halving (card 20+) is applied at runtime from live store state — not a stale snapshot
BREAK_PROMISE flat damage (5 PV, unscaled) is documented and intentional — correct design
All 8 core narrative effects handle their state mutations with explicit store calls — no silent state drift on the critical path
unverified_areas: Life drain call site (main.ts lines > 120) — cannot confirm drain fires at pipeline step 1 from this audit
cards.json biome tag coverage for all 8 biomes — external file not audited
Full run loop await chain (card -> minigame -> effects -> return) — beyond audit scope of lines 1-120
store.getState().unlockOgham() — method existence in Store.ts not verified
next_steps: Implement UNLOCK_OGHAM in EffectEngine switch (HIGH priority — blocks ogham unlock content)
Read main.ts lines 120-300 to verify drain fires at pipeline step 1 and run loop has no deadlock
Audit /data/cards.json for biome tag coverage across all 8 biomes
Fix FULFILL_PROMISE reward to flat 5 anam (remove scaleAndCap call)
Decide disposition of ADD_KARMA / ADD_TENSION / PROGRESS_MISSION / SET_FLAG / ADD_TAG / REMOVE_TAG (reject vs future-flag)
